text: Setting clear goals at the start of a project is essential for success. A useful method is to create SMART goals, which means they are specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, and time-bound. Instead of a vague aim like "finish the website soon," try "launch the main pages by the end of next month." Break your project into smaller milestones, such as completing a design draft or testing a prototype. Each milestone should have a concrete deliverable, like a report or a working feature. Finally, ensure stakeholder alignment by discussing these goals with everyone involved—clients, team members, and managers—so that all expectations are agreed upon from the beginning.
